👤 About Me

I am AmirHossein Movahedi, born on March 31, 2002, in Isfahan — known as the "Half of the World" — one of the largest and most historic cities in Iran. Currently, I study physics at university, where I've maintained a strong academic record.

I earned my bachelor's degree from Isfahan University of Technology, ranking 3rd among 60 students. This achievement earned me one of only five offers to pursue my master's degree at Sharif University of Technology, one of Iran's most prestigious institutions.

❤️ Passions & Hobbies

Beyond academics, I'm passionate about card games, which have deepened my understanding of probability theory and strategic thinking. Games serve as a bridge between theoretical mathematics and practical decision-making.

I'm driven by curiosity about how systems work — from neural networks to financial markets. The intersection of mathematics, physics, and biology fascinates me, and I'm committed to contributing meaningful research in these domains.

🧠 Research Interests

Complex Systems

Understanding intricate networks and emergent behaviors in biological and physical systems.

Brain Modeling

My master's thesis focuses on computational models of the human brain and neural dynamics.

Time Series Analysis

Exploring temporal patterns and predictive modeling in complex datasets.

Probability Theory

Deep dive into the mathematical foundations of uncertainty and probabilistic reasoning.

💻 Technical Skills

Python
R

Latex
